rulings.Case Title: TWIN CITY FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. WELLS FARGO BANK LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Resetting Joint Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltff Robin Jeanie Smith
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL SERVICES OF AMERICA GP, INC.'s Joint Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Robin Jeanie Smith Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Plaintiff shall appear for a continued deposition at a mutually agreed upon date no later than September 12, 2014. Plaintiff is ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $350 to UPS by September 12, 2014. All other requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN MAK et al VS. M.S.W. ENTERPRISES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 8: CROSS DEFENDANT DANIEL LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication. The court grants and denies the motion for summary judgment and adjudication in part. The moving party has maintained its summary judgment burden on its cross-complaint. The declaration of Daniel Lee shows that MSW owes $41,046.23. In opposition, the declaration of Ms. Wong (MSW's former president) creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the moving party properly performed the accounting services. Therefore, the motion is denied as to issues 1-5. The court grants the motion for summary adjudication as to issues 6-20. MSW's responses to the moving party's state all facts interrogatories are factually devoid. MSW merely parrots the allegations of the complaint. In opposition, MSW fails to create a triable issue of material fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL C. WONG VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 10: DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the age discrimination cause of action is overruled. Paragraph 16 of the amended complaint alleges that plaintiff's employment was terminated because of his age. The demurrer to the retaliation (CFRA) cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that he was retaliated against for providing information regarding family care per Cal. Gov't Code sec. 12945.2(l). Defendant's papers do not address that theory of liability. The demurrer to the wrongful termination cause of action is overruled because the FEHA and CFRA public policies can support the claim. The demurrers to causes of action 3, 4, and 5 (wage and contract claims) are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead the terms of the contract and entitlement to recovery with more detail. The demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not plead reasonable reliance. The demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff's allegations regarding age discrimination and retaliation support the claim and those allegations are not barred by worker's compensation exclusivity. See Fretland v. County of Humboldt, (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1492. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because the complaint alleges intentional misconduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: D CAMERON BAKER VS. PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 12: CROSS DEFENDANT D BAKER'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, Cross-Complainant is given leave to attach the contract that was inadvertently not attached to the Cross-Complaint. As to the second cause of action for fraud, Cross-Complainant is given leave to allege the elements of intent, justifiable reliance, and damages. Cross-Complainant is also granted leave to amend to allege the fraud cause of action with the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, holding that the particularity requirement for fraud "necessitates pleading facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.") As to the third and fourth causes of action, Cross-Complainant is given leave to allege facts establishing that Cross-Complainant acted to its detriment because of the alleged duress and undue influence, in a way that can be reconciled with the statement in the Cross-Complaint that Cross-Complainant "refused" to give in to Cross-Defendant's alleged demands. (Cross-Complaint, par. 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE HARRIS VS. DISCOUNT BEAUTY SUPPLY AKA BEAUTY SUPPLY WARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Disocvery Responses And Respone To Req. For Statement Of Damages And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 28, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT BEAUTY SUPPLY WAREHOUSE's Motion To Compel Disocvery Responses And Respone To Request For Statement Of Damages And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Plaintiff shall respond to all outstanding discovery within 15 days after notice of entry of this order. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions in the amount of $350 to Defendant no later than August 22, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

TAKATA CORPORATION DBA RESTORATION MANAGEMENT COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-517855 WITH CASE CGC-14-538848. Motion denied.
